A Sri Lanka Journey

Mists, Waves and Colonial Charm

Welcome to Ceylon

From the mist-draped tea highlands of Hatton to the cerulean waters lapping Tangalle's shores, and finally to the storied cobblestones of Galle's Dutch Fort — this nine-day journey through Sri Lanka is an unhurried immersion in three of the island's most distinctive worlds.

✦

Savour all-inclusive days at Ceylon Tea Trails, the world's first tea bungalow resort, before surrendering to the barefoot luxury of Amanwella on the southern coast. The journey culminates within the UNESCO-listed ramparts of Galle, where colonial grandeur and contemporary charm converge at the legendary Amangalla.

Signature Experiences

Highlights

Tea Experience at Dunkeld Factory — from leaf to cup in the highlands
Kayaking at Castlereagh Reservoir — paddling through mist-laden mountain scenery
Guided Trail Exploration with a Tea Trails Naturalist through endemic biodiversity

01–03
Days 01–03 · Tea Country

Hatton — Ceylon Tea Trails

AI
Day 01: Arrival — Hatton Ayubowan! Welcome to Sri Lanka. Upon arrival at Bandaranaike International Airport, your OV Holidays representative will greet you and escort you through immigration to the Silk Route Lounge, where you can relax, freshen up, and enjoy a refreshing cup of tea while your luggage is collected. Your dedicated chauffeur guide will then transfer you to Hatton.
Travel info: Airport — Hatton | 133 km | Approx. 4 hours 30 minutes
For a more exclusive and breathtaking journey, opt for a helicopter transfer or Cinnamon Air Domestic flight — soaking in Sri Lanka's stunning landscapes from a spectacular bird's-eye view. Approximate duration by air: 30 minutes. Arrive and check in at Ceylon Tea Trails — the world's first tea bungalow resort, perched at 4,000 feet in Sri Lanka's panoramic Ceylon tea region, bordering the World Heritage Central Highlands. This is the ideal setting to unwind and observe life on a working tea estate against the breath-taking backdrop of mountains, lakes, winding roads and waterfalls. Your personal butler will ensure there is plenty to do and explore during your time here.
Day 02: Tea Experience at the Bungalow After breakfast, proceed on your private Tea Experience at the Dunkeld Tea Factory, starting at 9 am. The Resident Tea Planter will demonstrate the age-old tea-making process — from tracing the trails of the planters of old Ceylon through dense jungle, to watching tea pluckers harvest two leaves and a bud with deft hands, to the factory where the conversion to "made" black tea takes place using machinery of traditional British design, some as old as 100 years. The experience ends with a tasting of different grades of this great brew. Note: Plucking of tea leaves is not done on public holidays and Sundays. A factory tour explaining the process will be arranged instead. Optional this afternoon — Embark on a kayaking adventure across the stunning Castlereagh Reservoir. Enjoy magnificent views of tea-carpeted mountains as you paddle in hi-spec kayaks. Mornings are most beautiful when the sun breaks through to gleam over the glassy water and the foreboding mountains emerge from the thinning mist.
Day 03: Guided Trail Exploration Accompanied by a Tea Trails Naturalist or Trail Guide, embark on a walking or cycling adventure through the scenic trails of Sri Lanka's tea country. The hillside terrain boasts rich biodiversity — tea plantations interwoven with forest patches sheltering endemic amphibians, vibrant birdlife, and elusive leopards. Your Naturalist will share fascinating insights into the region's flora and fauna, the daily life on a working tea estate, and captivating stories of its history and heritage. Excursion Duration: 1–3 hours After a day of trekking, relax at your bungalow with a warm cup of tea, enjoying the peaceful evening views. A cool breeze, good food, and cosy comfort make for the perfect end to the day.
Hotel
Ceylon Tea Trails
Garden Suite · Hatton · All Inclusive
04–06
Days 04–06 · Southern Coast

Tangalle — Amanwella

B
Day 04: Hatton — Tangalle Breakfast at the bungalow and check out to leave for your beach stay on Sri Lanka's southern coast.
Travel info: Hatton — Tangalle | 171 km | Approx. 5 hours
For a more exclusive journey, opt for a helicopter transfer to the resort — soaring over Sri Lanka's stunning landscapes from a spectacular bird's-eye view. Approximate duration by air: 30 minutes plus a 15-minute drive to the resort. Arrival and check in at Amanwella, Tangalle. The rest of the day is yours to spend at leisure.
Days 05 & 06: At Leisure Breakfast at the hotel. Full days to be spent at leisure at Amanwella. You are free to explore a private yoga session in the coconut grove, enjoy a personalized training session, or surrender to a rejuvenating spa treatment at the resort. At Amanwella, wellness is a deeply personalized journey, allowing guests to align mind, body, and spirit in a setting of unparalleled natural beauty. Wake with the sun and step into a sanctuary of stillness where your private yoga instructor awaits. Sessions can be held on the pristine beach — where the rhythmic waves set the pace for breathwork — or in a secluded jungle glade, surrounded by the gentle hum of nature. For those seeking an invigorating workout, Amanwella offers personalized training sessions designed to align with individual goals. Whether focusing on strength, endurance, flexibility, or overall well-being, your dedicated trainer curates a dynamic routine that maximizes results while embracing the natural environment. A 60-minute Amanwella signature massage for two is included once during your stay. A Mulgirigala rock temple tour is also on offer for those wishing to venture beyond the resort.
Hotel
Amanwella
Garden Pool Suite · Tangalle · Bed & Breakfast
07–08
Days 07–08 · Colonial Galle

Galle — Amangalla

B
Day 07: Tangalle — Galle This morning you will spend at leisure before departing for Galle — a historic city with a charming blend of colonial architecture, cobblestone streets, and coastal beauty.
Travel info: Tangalle — Galle | 74 km | Approx. 1 hour 20 minutes
A visit to the Handunugoda Tea Plantation is included during the inter-journey from Amanwella to Amangalla. Arrival and check in at Amangalla. Later this afternoon, explore the Galle Dutch Fort with your personal butler. Galle Fort Walk Set out in the late afternoon for an unhurried walk through Galle Fort, accompanied by your personal butler. This UNESCO World Heritage Site traces its origins to 1505, when the Portuguese established a trading post along Sri Lanka's southern coast. The Fort later came under Dutch control in the 17th century, with imposing ramparts and bastions built by 1663, followed by British influences that still shape its character today. Within the walls, narrow streets unfold past colonial residences, churches, mosques, and courtyards reflecting centuries of layered history. As evening approaches, join local residents in a time-honoured ritual — an easy stroll along the ramparts as the sun dips into the Indian Ocean. A traditional shave (for him) and a sandalwood facial (for her) are included once during the stay at Amangalla.
Day 08: Wheels and Meals Breakfast at the hotel. Sri Lanka's southern coast is a vibrant tapestry of nature, serene beaches, rich village life, and deep-rooted culture. The best way to immerse yourself in its beauty is on two wheels. Your journey begins at a tranquil lakefront, where you'll meet your cycling guide and enjoy a refreshing king coconut before setting off through a captivating mix of contemporary and rural villages, weaving past emerald-green paddy fields and scenic plantations of coconut, cinnamon, and tea. The ride culminates at a delightful village home near the beach, where the second part of your adventure begins — a hands-on Sri Lankan cooking experience with your hostess, Dinesha. Sourcing herbs and spices from her own garden, she will guide you through the art of preparing traditional Sri Lankan dishes. Step into her semi-outdoor kitchen and dive into a sensory journey of grinding spices, tempering curries, and balancing flavours. After the class, you'll savour the delicious meal in a serene setting, leaving with not just a collection of recipes, but a deeper connection to Sri Lanka's rich food culture.
Hotel
Amangalla
Bedroom · Galle Fort · Bed & Breakfast
09
Day 09 · Farewell

Galle — Airport

B
Breakfast at the hotel. Leave for the airport in time for your departure flight.
Travel info: Galle — Airport | 149 km | Approx. 2 hours 15 minutes
The exclusive Gold Lounge at Colombo Airport will be reserved for you, ensuring a seamless and luxurious pre-flight experience. End of Services
Hotel
On Board
Airport Transfer · Colombo

Before You Go

Important Notes

Poya Days
Alcohol service is restricted on religious holidays (Poya Days). Alcohol is not available for purchase at bars or restaurants on these dates. Please contact the reception for guidance during these periods.
Check-In & Check-Out
Standard hotel check-in is 14:00. Standard check-out is 12:00. Early check-in and late check-out are subject to availability and may incur additional fees.
Belongings
Hotel management is not responsible for lost, stolen, or damaged personal belongings during the tour. We recommend using in-room safes and exercising care with valuables.
Travel Times
All travel times are approximate and subject to traffic conditions, road works, and weather. Please allow flexibility in your schedule.
Gratuities
Gratuities for guides and drivers are at your discretion. We recommend presenting tips in an envelope at the end of your journey as a token of appreciation.
Dress Code
Light, breathable clothing is recommended. Comfortable walking shoes are essential. At religious sites, please dress modestly — covering from knees to elbows. Men in shorts and ladies in short skirts may not be permitted entry to some sites.
